org.arakhne.neteditor.fig.figure.edge.symbol

Class SlotInEdgeSymbol

  • java.lang.Object
    • org.arakhne.neteditor.formalism.AbstractPropertyTooler
      • org.arakhne.neteditor.fig.figure.edge.symbol.EdgeSymbol
        • org.arakhne.neteditor.fig.figure.edge.symbol.AbstractFillableEdgeSymbol
          • org.arakhne.neteditor.fig.figure.edge.symbol.SlotInEdgeSymbol
  • All Implemented Interfaces:
    Serializable, PropertyNames

    public class SlotInEdgeSymbol
extends AbstractFillableEdgeSymbol
    This class represents the "slot-in" symbols that will be paint in the ends of edges.


    Fig: Example of a SlotInEdgeSymbol, which is not filled
    Version:
    17.0 2013-10-23 21:03:01
    Author:
    Stéphane GALLAND
    See Also:
    Serialized Form
    Maven Group Id:
    org.arakhne.neteditor
    Maven Artifact Id:
    neteditor-fig

    • Constructor Detail

      • SlotInEdgeSymbol

        public SlotInEdgeSymbol(float width,
                float height,
                boolean filled)
        Construct a new slot-in.
        Parameters:
        width - is the size of the rectangle perpendicularly to the edge vector.
        height - is the size of the rectangle along the edge vector.
        filled - indicates how the symbol is filled. If true the color replied by EdgeSymbol.getFillingColor() is used. If false the color replied by EdgeSymbol.getLineColor() is used.

      • SlotInEdgeSymbol

        public SlotInEdgeSymbol()
        Construct a new slot-in.

      • SlotInEdgeSymbol

        public SlotInEdgeSymbol(float width,
                float height)
        Construct a new slot-in.
        Parameters:
        width - is the size of the rectangle perpendicularly to the edge vector.
        height - is the size of the rectangle along the edge vector.

      • SlotInEdgeSymbol

        public SlotInEdgeSymbol(boolean filled)
        Construct a new slot-in.
        Parameters:
        filled - indicates how the symbol is filled. If true the color replied by EdgeSymbol.getFillingColor() is used. If false the color replied by EdgeSymbol.getLineColor() is used.

    • Method Detail

      • getWidth

        public float getWidth()
        Reply the size of the slot-in perpendicularly to the ege vector.
        Returns:
        the width of the slot-in.

      • getHeight

        public float getHeight()
        Reply the size of the slot-in along the ege vector.
        Returns:
        the height of the slot-in.

      • getSymbolShape

        protected Shape2f getSymbolShape(float x,
                     float y,
                     float angle)
        Compute and replies the shape of the symbol.
        Specified by:
        getSymbolShape in class EdgeSymbol
        Parameters:
        x - is the position of the symbol anchor.
        y - is the position of the symbol anchor.
        angle - is the angle of the symbol.
        Returns:
        the shape.
